title: Baseline Cloudflare preview gateway returned 500 on contact pages

evidence.summary: Baseline preview gateway returned 500 for /en/contact and /zh/contact while /en, /zh, and /api/health returned 200.
<redacted-web-preview-worker> records an exception and logs Next Cache Components blocking-route for /[locale]/contact; /en/products in the same tail has no exception.
Gateway worker receives the contact requests as Ok, localizing the failure to the web worker render path.
next dev and next start controls both return 200 for /en/contact and /zh/contact.
The contact route registers contactFormAction into its page server-reference manifest; the form hook binds it with useActionState and the action reads request headers when executed.

impact: At baseline, the preview contact page was the main lead-generation runtime blocker. Repair-wave deployed smoke now passes both contact routes.
root_cause: Confirmed root-cause class: Cloudflare/OpenNext trips Next Cache Components blocking-route while rendering /[locale]/contact. The exact source line is not yet source-mapped because Cloudflare tail stack is minified and next build --debug-prerender did not reproduce the error. Strongest correlated boundary is the contact route's Server Action/PPR setup and incomplete full-page Suspense isolation compared with other async content pages.
recommended_fix: Keep deployed preview smoke as a permanent route proof for both contact locales. Do not claim real form delivery until Resend/Airtable/Turnstile credentials are present in a safe staging or production environment.
verification_needed: Preview route smoke must continue returning 200 for /en/contact and /zh/contact. Real form delivery still needs separate Resend/Airtable/Turnstile secrets before it can be claimed.

title: Buyer-facing phone number is still placeholder-like

evidence.summary: Canonical contact config sets phone to +86-518-0000-0000.
Contact page renders siteFacts.contact.phone into the buyer-facing contact card.
Terms page publishes the same placeholder-like phone number.

impact: A buyer may treat the company as unfinished or unverified at the moment they are deciding whether to inquire.
root_cause: Launch fact validation does not catch numeric placeholder patterns such as 0000-0000.
recommended_fix: Verify the real sales phone number and replace it, or remove phone from public surfaces until the owner confirms it. Add a launch check for 0000/example-style contact placeholders.
verification_needed: Run a placeholder scan and visit /en/contact and /en/terms to confirm the displayed phone is real or intentionally absent.

title: ISO certificate path is declared but the public PDF is missing

evidence.summary: Site facts declare ISO 9001 certificate number 240021Q09730R0S and file path /certs/iso9001.pdf.
test -e public/certs/iso9001.pdf returned missing.
Homepage quality copy displays ISO 9001 certification and related buyer-trust claims.

impact: The site asks procurement buyers to trust a certificate claim while the declared proof file does not exist.
root_cause: Business proof metadata was added before the actual public proof asset, and no asset-existence gate checks siteFacts proof files.
recommended_fix: Either add the approved certificate PDF and link it intentionally, or remove the file path and keep certificate proof request-based until publication is approved.
verification_needed: Confirm public/certs/iso9001.pdf exists and is intentionally referenced, or confirm no dead certificate path remains after cleanup.

title: Live product families still use sample placeholder images

evidence.summary: The SVG visibly says Sample Product and Replace this image with your real product photo.
North America product families reference /images/products/sample-product.svg.
Lane 04 enumerates additional live product spec files that use the sample placeholder image.

impact: Product pages cannot visually prove many products to overseas buyers; this directly weakens trust and inquiry conversion.
root_cause: Placeholder media remains in the typed product spec source without a launch gate.
recommended_fix: Replace sample images with real product photos or product-specific technical illustrations. If media is not ready, reduce or hide affected product cards instead of showing obvious placeholders.
verification_needed: Static scan returns zero live product-spec references to sample-product.svg, and product pages show product-specific media.

title: AS/NZS standard wording conflicts across public copy and product data

evidence.summary: Homepage standards copy labels AS/NZS as AS/NZS 61386.
Product FAQ asks about AS/NZS 61386 and describes it as IEC-style.
Canonical Australia/New Zealand product catalog labels the market as AS/NZS 2053.
Product specs and certifications also use AS/NZS 2053.

impact: A compliance-focused buyer can read the site as making two different standards claims, which hurts trust and SEO clarity.
root_cause: Standards vocabulary is duplicated across translations, MDX FAQ, product catalog, and typed specs.
recommended_fix: Choose one approved standards vocabulary and derive or validate homepage copy, FAQ, catalog labels, and product specs from it.
verification_needed: Run rg -n "AS/NZS" src content messages and confirm only approved buyer-facing wording remains.

title: Product inquiry tests claim real validation while mocking the schema

evidence.summary: Test header claims JSON parsing and validation run as real code.
The same integration test mocks productLeadSchema.safeParse to always succeed.
Production /api/inquiry validation branch depends on productLeadSchema.safeParse.
The route unit test also mocks productLeadSchema.safeParse to always succeed.

impact: The main product inquiry path can lose route-level validation proof while the test suite stays green.
root_cause: Tests mock internal validation instead of only external services, then overstate what they prove.
recommended_fix: Remove the productLeadSchema mock from route/integration tests, keep external services mocked, and add invalid inquiry requests proving the route returns INQUIRY_VALIDATION_FAILED before processLead.
verification_needed: Run targeted inquiry route/integration tests with the real schema, then run pnpm test:lead-family and pnpm test.

title: Product market route owns too many responsibilities

evidence.summary: One route file imports market specs, owns spec registry, metadata, JSON-LD, cache boundary, and render path.
Product market page is a large production file and exceeds the project 500-line target.

impact: Adding or changing a product market requires edits across route code, catalog, specs, translations, SEO, sitemap, and tests.
root_cause: The route file is acting as both controller and product-domain registry.
recommended_fix: Move market/spec registration and page-model shaping into a product-market domain module; delete the page-local SPECS_BY_MARKET map.
verification_needed: Simulate adding one market and prove route imports do not need editing; run product page tests, sitemap/static params tests, and pnpm dep:check.

title: Locale and message truth is split across hardcoded two-locale maps

evidence.summary: next-intl routing declares en/zh and defaultLocale.
Path config repeats locale list, default locale, prefixes, display names, and time zones.
Contact page directly imports en/zh message files and owns a page-local static locale map.

impact: Adding a third language is a scavenger hunt and can leave partial routing, content loading, or contact-page behavior.
root_cause: Locale metadata is not fully centralized; routing, path config, content config, message loading, and page-local loaders can drift.
recommended_fix: Pick one locale registry and derive routing/content/path/message loading from it, or generate page-local maps from that source.
verification_needed: Use a test-only fake locale to prove TypeScript/tests point to one source of required updates; run i18n parity checks.

title: Contact copy can silently fall back to old underConstruction namespace

evidence.summary: Contact copy loader reads contact first, then underConstruction.pages.contact.
Fresh legacy marker audit points to the contact fallback.
Production message files still carry underConstruction.pages.contact copy.

impact: Missing current contact copy can be hidden by old launch copy instead of failing loudly on the lead-generation page.
root_cause: A migration compatibility fallback remained in production after contact became a real page.
recommended_fix: Delete the production underConstruction fallback; move any old-copy behavior to test fixtures only.
verification_needed: Remove/rename a required contact key in a fixture and verify contact copy tests fail instead of falling back; run review:legacy-markers and contact tests.

title: Static route truth is duplicated across route config, next-intl, sitemap, and CTA literals

evidence.summary: Route path tables and next-intl pathnames repeat static and dynamic routes.
Sitemap config repeats public static paths and concrete product market URLs.
CTA route literals are repeated in page-expression config.

impact: Renaming or deleting one page can leave navigation, sitemap, language switching, or tests out of sync.
root_cause: Explicit route config exists, but multiple literal route lists still survive instead of deriving from one typed registry.
recommended_fix: Make PATHS_CONFIG the only static-route registry and derive pathnames, sitemap paths, and CTA hrefs from route IDs.
verification_needed: Rename or remove one route in a repair branch and verify one compile/test surface catches all required follow-ups; run route tests, sitemap tests, truth:check, and dep:check.

title: Subscribe public write path has weaker validation order and weaker tests than contact/inquiry

evidence.summary: Subscribe checks only email presence before Turnstile and sends final validation to processLead.
Newsletter email schema validation exists but runs after route-level Turnstile work.
Fresh search found widget assertions but no /api/subscribe route-level security tests.

impact: Bad newsletter requests can spend external verification budget before cheap validation, and regressions in subscribe protections are less likely to be caught.
root_cause: Subscribe reused the shared lead pipeline but did not get the same route-level validation and proof contract as contact/inquiry.
recommended_fix: Add a small route-level subscribe schema before Turnstile and add subscribe API tests for invalid JSON, body size, idempotency, rate limit, Turnstile, invalid email, and happy path.
verification_needed: Run the new subscribe route tests plus existing API security tests.

title: Baseline accessibility proof had false-green browser checks

evidence.summary: Baseline checkA11y called builder.analyze() but did not assert violations and ignored context/options.
Homepage and navigation tests called the baseline helper, so they could pass despite axe violations.
Baseline contact form ARIA assertion used hasAriaDescribedBy !== null || true, which always passed.

impact: At baseline, the project could believe lead-page and navigation accessibility were protected when browser tests were not actually failing on violations.
root_cause: A11y helpers and smoke assertions were left as wrappers instead of executable behavior checks.
recommended_fix: Keep checkA11y asserting unapproved violations and applying context/options; do not reintroduce tautological contact-form ARIA assertions.
verification_needed: Run tests/unit/e2e-helpers/axe.test.ts plus relevant Playwright accessibility/contact smoke tests when browser runtime is available.
repair_status: Fixed in repair wave; checkA11y now filters/asserts violations and selector-scoped calls pass context/options.

title: Unused-code check is green but configured too broadly to prove dead-code reachability

evidence.summary: pnpm unused:check passed.
Knip entry/project config broadly includes src/components and src/lib, weakening reachability proof.
A copy-only template lives under src/components and should be explicitly ignored or moved out of production source.

impact: Green unused-code output can hide stale component/lib files, making cleanup more expensive.
root_cause: False positives were avoided by treating too much production source as an entrypoint.
recommended_fix: Narrow Knip entries to true app/router/config/script roots and explicitly ignore documented templates; let components/libs be checked through imports.
verification_needed: Run pnpm unused:check and pnpm unused:production after config tightening, then inspect surfaced files before deletion or Trash-first cleanup.

title: Baseline mutation freshness guard could recommend a missing package script

evidence.summary: Baseline guard could recommend pnpm test:mutation:lead-security when lead-pipeline and security are both touched.
Baseline unit test locked in the same missing command recommendation.
Baseline mutation scripts did not include test:mutation:lead-security.

impact: At baseline, a high-risk future branch could get a mutation instruction the team could not run.
root_cause: The helper's suggested command drifted from package.json and tests verified the drift.
recommended_fix: Keep the combined lead-security mutation script and the unit test that validates suggested commands against package.json scripts.
verification_needed: Run tests/unit/scripts/check-mutation-required.test.ts and node scripts/check-mutation-required.js.
repair_status: Fixed in repair wave; package.json now includes test:mutation:lead-security using Stryker's comma-separated mutate list.

title: Mobile and touch controls are denser than common 44px tap targets

evidence.summary: Shared button sizes include h-[38px], h-8, h-10, and size-9.
Mobile menu and language links use compact px-3 py-2 text-sm rows.
Product family jump links use px-3 py-1.5 text-sm.

impact: Mobile buyers have less forgiving tap areas in menus and product navigation, increasing mis-taps and friction.
root_cause: Shared UI defaults optimize desktop density and are reused in mobile-critical surfaces.
recommended_fix: Ensure mobile-critical controls have at least 44px clickable boxes while keeping visual density if needed.
verification_needed: Run browser-level mobile viewport checks for /en, /en/products, /en/products/north-america, and /en/contact.

title: Product-market pages use generic contact CTA instead of product-specific inquiry context

evidence.summary: Market pages render one bottom CTA.
Market landing CTA target is /contact.
Product-specific quote drawer exists but is not wired into live product market pages.

impact: Interested buyers leave product context and must retype what they want, reducing quote quality and conversion.
root_cause: Product-specific inquiry UI exists as an isolated component but not as part of the live product page flow.
recommended_fix: Either wire product/family inquiry actions into live product pages with prefilled context, or delete unused product-specific inquiry components if launch scope is generic contact only.
verification_needed: Visit product market pages and confirm family-level quote actions preserve product context, or confirm unused inquiry components are removed.

title: Production CSP still allows inline script elements without runtime proof that it is unavoidable

evidence.summary: Production script-src-elem includes 'unsafe-inline'.
Runtime CSP check was blocked in Lane 02 because it needed a production build and the lane could not run build.

impact: CSP is weaker as a last-resort defense if an HTML/script injection bug appears.
root_cause: A compatibility exception is broad and permanent rather than tied to measured required inline framework scripts.
recommended_fix: After a build is available, run CSP proof. Remove unsafe-inline if not needed, or document the exact unavoidable scripts and prefer nonce/hash allowance.
verification_needed: Run pnpm security:csp:check after pnpm build and inspect representative page CSP headers.

title: Next.js middleware deprecation is real but cannot be blindly migrated because Cloudflare proof depends on it

evidence.summary: pnpm build passed but emitted the middleware file convention deprecation warning.
pnpm build:cf passed and emitted the same warning.
Project rule says src/middleware.ts is the Cloudflare-compatible entry and src/proxy.ts blocks build:cf.

impact: No buyer impact today, but future Next.js upgrades will keep pressuring this boundary.
root_cause: Next.js prefers proxy while the repo currently records a Cloudflare/OpenNext constraint around middleware.
recommended_fix: Do not rename during audit. Create a separate proof plan for any middleware/proxy migration.
verification_needed: Fresh pnpm build, pnpm build:cf, preview:cf, and deployed smoke after any migration.

title: Cache tag module keeps unused product/content/SEO tag families

evidence.summary: Runtime tag invalidation and cacheTag are out of the current launch architecture.
Cache tag module defines i18n, content, product, and SEO tag families.
Production search shows only i18nTags used.

impact: Future changes can mistake unused future-facing cache tags for supported runtime invalidation.
root_cause: Cache tag system survived a runtime-cache rollback.
recommended_fix: Delete unused content/product/SEO tag families or move them to an archived design note; keep only what production uses.
verification_needed: Run rg for contentTags/productTags/seoTags/cacheTags and pnpm dep:check after cleanup.

title: One production module still imports through a barrel against the dependency rule

evidence.summary: Dependency-cruiser reports warn no-barrel-export-dependencies for src/lib/utm.ts -> src/lib/cookie-consent/index.ts.
utm imports loadConsent through the cookie-consent barrel.

impact: Small coupling smell; a low-level attribution utility can accidentally pull more cookie-consent surface than needed.
root_cause: Convenience import beat the existing dependency rule.
recommended_fix: Import loadConsent directly from the storage module or narrow the barrel.
verification_needed: Run pnpm dep:check and confirm zero dependency-cruiser warnings.

title: CSP report endpoint logs several attacker-controlled fields

evidence.summary: CSP report log payload includes browser-provided fields; URLs and IPs are normalized and scriptSample is truncated.
Existing PII lint passed, so this is observability hardening rather than a detected PII violation.

impact: Attacker-influenced telemetry can add noisy or sensitive snippets to production logs.
root_cause: The endpoint favors debugging detail over a strict logging allowlist.
recommended_fix: Log only directive, blocked URL origin/path, status code, redacted user-agent family, and a hash or safe fragment of script sample.
verification_needed: Add CSP report tests with query strings, control characters, and script samples; assert logs do not contain raw sensitive fields.

title: Hero entrance animation exists before runtime performance impact is proven

evidence.summary: Hero title, subtitle, CTA row, proof strip, and visual grid use hero-stagger classes.
Hero stagger classes apply 700-800ms opacity/transform animations with delays.
Reduced-motion support exists.

impact: The first viewport may feel slower, but no Lighthouse/Web Vitals damage was proven in this run.
root_cause: Above-the-fold animation is a default visual pattern without performance proof.
recommended_fix: Keep unless runtime data shows damage; if needed, shorten/remove above-fold stagger while keeping lower-priority reveal effects.
verification_needed: Run Lighthouse or Playwright Web Vitals before/after on /en and /zh.

title: UI primitive tests are over-expanded relative to buyer-flow proof

evidence.summary: There are 97 UI primitive test files.
There are 23 direct UI primitive source files.
The wider test suite contains many surface-rendering or weak assertion patterns.

impact: Review time can drift toward low-level permutations while higher-value buyer flows need stronger proof.
root_cause: AI-style exhaustive matrix generation around primitives instead of tighter behavior contracts around real pages.
recommended_fix: Stop adding primitive permutation tests by default; consolidate duplicates when touched and shift new proof budget to buyer flows and route-level contracts.
verification_needed: After cleanup, run pnpm test, compare runtime/count, and ensure buyer-flow suites still pass.
